differential_diagnoses: Intracranial hypertension secondary to cirrhosis (or other causes)
next_steps: Perform Brain MRI with MR Venography (MRV) and contrast-enhanced MRA to rule out venous thrombosis and arterial dissection
Ophthalmologic examination with fundoscopy to assess for papilledema or evidence of retinal vasculitis
Check current coagulation profile (INR, PTT, fibrinogen) and platelet count
Measure ESR and CRP to screen for Giant-cell arteritis
Perform lumbar puncture for opening pressure and CSF analysis (after ruling out mass effect via MRI)
Review all current medications for potential drug-induced or withdrawal-related headache triggers
Consult neurology for urgent assessment of new neurological deficits
